title: Realtime Channel
description: A canonical representation of a realtime channel — the named topic, room, queue, or stage to which clients publish and from which they subscribe. Generalizes the channel concepts found in Ably, Pusher, PubNub, Socket.IO rooms/namespaces, MQTT topics, Phoenix Channels, GraphQL subscription topics, and LiveKit rooms.

id.description: A globally unique identifier for the channel within a provider tenant — typically a UUID, an opaque token, or a provider-scoped string.

name.description: The human-readable channel name as it appears in client subscribe/publish calls (e.g., 'chat:room-42', 'orders/123/events', 'sensors/+/temperature').

namespace.description: Optional namespace or scope grouping multiple channels (e.g., an Ably namespace prefix, a Socket.IO namespace, a tenant identifier).

protocol.description: The underlying realtime protocol carrying messages on this channel.

provider.description: Identifier for the realtime provider or platform hosting the channel (e.g., 'ably', 'pubnub', 'pusher', 'livekit', 'socket.io', 'mqtt-broker').

type.description: The functional role of the channel.

qualityOfService.description: Delivery guarantee for messages on the channel, modeled on MQTT QoS plus the common at-least-once/exactly-once labels.

persistence.description: Whether and how messages published to this channel are retained for replay or history.

enabled.description: True if message history is retained.

retention.description: Retention period expressed as an ISO 8601 duration (e.g., 'PT24H', 'P7D').

maxMessages.description: Maximum number of messages retained, if bounded by count rather than time.

enabled.description: True if the channel tracks present members.

memberCount.description: Current number of present members, when known.

access.description: Access control posture for the channel.

encryption.description: End-to-end or in-flight encryption applied to messages on the channel.

inFlight.description: True when transport-level encryption (TLS/DTLS/SRTP) is in use.

endToEnd.description: True when payload-level encryption is applied above the transport.

labels.description: Free-form key/value labels for tenancy, environment, application, etc.
